name: quilt
description: Build and check school class schedules through chat — place each grade group into class periods and instantly catch double-bookings and other conflicts.
long_description: Quilt lets you build a school's weekly class schedule just by talking to Claude. Put each grade group (cohort) into the right class and time slot, and Quilt automatically flags problems — a grade booked for two classes at once, a missing or unknown grade, a traveling teacher who can't make it between rooms in time, or a class scheduled during a grade's lunch or recess. Already have a schedule in Excel? Quilt imports your existing workbook so you don't start over. When you're done, Quilt can draw a live, color-coded schedule grid — a whole-school view or a single class's week — that updates as you make changes, with conflicts highlighted in red.

tools.name: board
tools.description: Show the full schedule grid with every conflict highlighted.

tools.name: import
tools.description: Bring an existing Excel schedule workbook into Quilt.

tools.name: assign
tools.description: Put a grade group into a class at a specific time slot.

tools.name: validate
tools.description: Check the schedule and list every conflict it finds.

prompts.name: live_view
prompts.description: Draw a live, color-coded schedule grid that updates as you make changes.
prompts.text: Draw a live, color-coded view of the schedule that refreshes itself as I make changes, with any conflicts highlighted in red. Read the view:// HTML skeleton resource and the live-views guide, then render the `board` payload. Ask me whether I want the whole-school view (every class) or a single class's week.

prompts.name: migrate_xlsx
prompts.description: Bring an existing Excel schedule into Quilt so you don't start over.
prompts.text: Import my existing Excel schedule workbook into Quilt. Read the Excel-migration guide resource, ask me for the path to my .xlsx file, then use the `import` tool to bring it in and `validate` to show me any conflicts it found.

prompts.name: new_schedule
prompts.description: Start a brand-new schedule from scratch and fill it in step by step.
prompts.text: Help me build a brand-new school schedule from scratch. Read the schedule-model guide resource, ask me about my classes, grade groups, and time slots, then use the `assign` tool to place each grade group into its classes and `validate` to catch conflicts as we go.
